subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Solar Wind
description: A 60-second lesson on The Solar Wind.

hook.text: The Sun constantly blows a stream of charged particles outward at 400–800 km/s. This solar wind fills the solar system.

buildup.text: It's mostly protons and electrons from the Sun's corona. The wind carries the Sun's magnetic field outward with it.

discovery.text: Eugene Parker predicted the solar wind in 1958. Many doubted him, but spacecraft quickly confirmed his theory.

twist.text: The solar wind creates the heliosphere—a protective bubble around the solar system that deflects most galactic cosmic rays.

climax.text: Parker Solar Probe is flying through the corona itself—touching the Sun at just 6 million km from its surface.

punchline.text: A constant wind of particles from the Sun. It shields our entire solar system.

quiz.question: What is the heliosphere?
options: A protective bubble created by the solar wind that shields the solar system from galactic cosmic rays
The visible atmosphere of the Sun seen during solar eclipses
A region near the Sun where temperatures exceed one million degrees
